code: @Manual{,   title = {mighty.component: Mighty Components for ADaM Generation},   author = {Aksel Thomsen and Matthew Phelps},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9020},   url = {https://novonordisk-opensource.github.io/mighty.component}, }

title: Mighty Components for ADaM Generation
text: mighty.component serve repository generic compute component classes used produce ADaM scripts {mighty} framework.

title: Mighty Components for ADaM Generation
text: can install development version mighty.component GitHub :

title: Mighty Components for ADaM Generation
text: mighty.component provide generic classes work mighty components, helper functions retrieve . now also holds small temporary library components. See vignette("mighty-component") work components.

title: Retrieve mighty code component — get_component
text: Retrieve mighty code component local file. get_component(): Returns object class mighty_component. get_rendered_component(): Returns object class mighty_component_rendered. rendering component required list parameters depends individual component. Check documentation local component details.

title: Retrieve mighty code component — get_component
text: component character path component file (.R .mustache). params named list input parameters. Passed along mighty_component$render().

title: Retrieve mighty code component — get_component
text: Processes different component types based file extension: .R: Extracts renders custom functions. .mustache: Creates components template files.

title: Retrieve mighty code component — get_component
code: path <- system.file("examples", "ady.mustache", package = "mighty.component") get_component(path) #> <mighty_component/R6> #> /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/mighty.component/examples/ady.mustache: Derives #> the relative day compared to the treatment start date. #> Type: column #> Parameters: #> • domain: `character` Name of new domain being created #> • variable: `character` Name of new variable to create #> • date: `character` Name of date variable to use #> Depends: #> • {{domain}}.{{date}} #> • {{domain}}.TRTSDT #> Outputs: #> • {{ variable }}

what: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
title: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
text: Creates mighty_component_test object rendered component, enabling structured unit testing optional coverage checking. See mighty_component_test description testing workflow.

title: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
code: get_test_component(   component,   params = list(),   check_coverage = TRUE,   teardown_env = parent.frame() )

title: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
text: component character path component file (.R .mustache). params named list input parameters. Passed along mighty_component$render(). check_coverage logical(1) Whether automatically check test coverage test completes. TRUE (default), coverage verified via test_component$check_coverage() deferred call. teardown_env environment register deferred coverage check. Defaults caller's environment (parent.frame()). controls check_coverage() executes test teardown.

title: Create a testable component for unit testing — get_test_component
text: mighty_component_test object.

title: List components in directories — list_components
text: List available mighty components (.R .mustache files) specified directories.

title: List components in directories — list_components
text: path character vector directory paths search components. Format list components . Default character lists component IDs (filenames without extension), list tibble show detailed component metadata.

title: List components in directories — list_components
text: Depending : character: vector component IDs list: list component metadata (id, title, description, params, depends, outputs, code) tibble: tibble one row per component

title: List components in directories — list_components
code: path <- system.file("examples", package = "mighty.component") list_components(path) #> [1] "ady"  list_components(path, as = "list") |> str(max.level = 1) #> List of 1 #>  $ :List of 7

what: Internal parameters for reuse in functions — mighty.component-options-params
title: Internal parameters for reuse in functions — mighty.component-options-params
text: Internal parameters reuse functions

title: Internal parameters for reuse in functions — mighty.component-options-params
text: verbosity_level Verbosity level functions mighty.component. See zephyr::verbosity_level details.. Default: NA_character_.

title: Internal parameters for reuse in functions — mighty.component-options-params
text: See mighty.component-options information.

title: Options for mighty.component — mighty.component-options
text: Verbosity level functions mighty.component. See zephyr::verbosity_level details. Default: NA_character_ Option: mighty.component.verbosity_level Environment: R_MIGHTY.COMPONENT_VERBOSITY_LEVEL

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: Class generic mighty component. mighty framework, "component" code template processes input data returns modified version new columns rows. Mighty components share common structure roxygen-like documentation pattern, facilitating use inside mighty.

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: Templates character vectors R code interpreted. Dynamic use variables etc. supported using mustache framework. Dynamic parameters specified using {{ variable_name }}.

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: template required documented following tags similar documenting functions using roxygen2:

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: component template follows conventions: input data set always called {{ domain }}. Additional parameters used render template R code documented @param tag. template ends creating modified version {{ domain }}. Template documented roxygen-like tags

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: example mighty component template creates new dynamic variable variable twice value dynamic input x, already input data set {{ domain }}.   rendered parameters variable = "" x = "B" rendered code used mighty becomes:
code: #' @title Title for my component #' @description #' A more in depth description of what is being done #' #' @param variable dynamic output if applicable #' @param x some other input to the component #' @type column #' @origin Derived #' @depends {{ domain }} {{ x }} #' @outputs {{ variable }} #' @code {{ domain }} <- {{ domain }} |>   dplyr::mutate(     {{ variable }} = 2 * {{ x }}   ) {{ domain }} <- {{ domain }} |>   dplyr::mutate(     A = 2 * B   )

title: Mighty component — mighty_component
text: id Component ID. title Title component. description Description component. code code block component. template complete template. type type component. Can one column, row, parameter, internal. origin CDISC origin. One Assigned, Collected, Derived, Available, , Predecessor, Protocol NULL. depends Data.frame listing components dependencies. outputs List new columns created component. params Data.frame listing parameters need supplied rendering component.

title: Test mighty component — mighty_component_test
text: Class unit testing mighty component code coverage tracking. Runs component code isolated R session tracks lines executed testing.

title: Test mighty component — mighty_component_test
text: Always use get_test_component() create instances testing. test workflow : Create test component get_test_component() Assign input data $assign() Execute track coverage $eval() Retrieve results $get() Test results expect_*() functions {testthat} Coverage automatically checked test teardown via $check_coverage().

title: Test mighty component — mighty_component_test
text: mighty.component::mighty_component -> mighty.component::mighty_component_rendered -> mighty_component_test

title: Test mighty component — mighty_component_test
text: percent_coverage numeric Percentage lines covered (0-100). line_coverage data.frame columns line value showing execution count per line.
